published_prior_art.cite: DaouiaFlorensSimar2010FrontierEVT + boundary-tail variance bookkeeping, not CCOT PRTE
TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT gives CCOT PRTE rates, not the boundary-local experiment
KimPollard1990CubeRoot and DaouiaFlorensSimar2010FrontierEVT are adjacent boundary-limit precedents, not this alpha=1 PRTE process nonuniformity
TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT characterizes continuous-instrument rates but not this endpoint decomposition or phase-specific limit law
CaiLow2005AdaptiveCI and RobinsVanderVaart2006AdaptiveSets give abstract adaptation obstructions, not a CCOT PRTE two-alpha endpoint theorem
ImbensManski2004PartialCI and Stoye2009MorePartialCI cover regular partial-ID endpoint CI templates
FangSantos2019DirectionalInference and FranguridiLiu2025RegularizedOTPartialID suggest calibration routes but do not solve fixed-alpha unregularized CCOT PRTE CIs

soundness_flags.label: ass:strict-overlap-propensity
soundness_flags.one_line: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML supports binary-treatment propensity overlap, not the stated lower support-boundary condition underline_p(X) >= p_so for a continuous-IV generalized propensity map.

soundness_flags.label: thm:local-experiment and thm:endpoint-limit-law
soundness_flags.one_line: The assumptions give two-sided order bounds for boundary mass and weighted second moments, but no regular-variation or vague-convergence condition for the joint boundary shell law of (B, chi_P, active face), so the claimed unique Gaussian/log/Levy process is under-specified.

soundness_flags.label: prop:phase-normalization
soundness_flags.one_line: The weighted-loading-tail assumption controls an uncentered second moment, but the lower nondegenerate variance bound for S_nP also needs an explicit centered shell-variance or anti-cancellation condition.

soundness_flags.label: thm:endpoint-limit-law
soundness_flags.one_line: The boundary score excludes B = 0, while the assumptions do not rule out an atom at the lower support face or supply a separate boundary-atom residual term in the endpoint decomposition.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:unique-active-face and ass:dual-gap-scale
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The active-face uniqueness and first-order dual-gap geometry are assumed, not derived from the CCOT primitives.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All endpoint limit-law and adaptation claims are conditional on the unique-active-face, separated-dual-gap subclass.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:weighted-loading-tail
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The alpha-indexed weighted singular tail scale is imposed as a regime condition rather than derived from primitive smoothness of chi_P and B.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The phase-transition headline is a conditional field-tier claim under the imposed weighted-tail regime unless the revision derives the scale from more primitive conditions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the two-sided shell order assumptions with a regular-variation package for the joint law of (B/n, chi_P, active-face label), including the exact covariance or Levy-measure object used by H_P,alpha,u0.
Add either P(B = 0) = 0 or an explicit boundary-atom residual term and corresponding limit component in S_nP and the endpoint decomposition.
Retag ass:strict-overlap-propensity as novel or cite a PRTE/MTE/continuous-IV support-overlap source that actually imposes a lower support floor for the generalized propensity map.
Strengthen the phase-normalization proposition with a centered shell-variance nondegeneracy condition so the lower variance bound is not inferred from an uncentered second moment alone.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the novelty survives at field tier, but the boundary-shell limit assumptions and strict-overlap citation need repair before Stage 0.
